_meta.title: Deconstructed: How the Masters Composed the Frame
_meta.description: Chapter-per-photographer deconstruction. Each photograph gets an overlaid compositional diagram, prose on why it works, and camera settings where recorded.

decisions.rightsPosture: fair-use / educational commentary; takedown risk accepted by owner
decisions.overlayMethod: TBD — programmatic-first vs hand-authored (gates deep-research output format)

_meta.note: status is a SOURCING and takedown-likelihood heuristic, not legal advice. Dates and PD calls are best-known and get verified per-image in the deep-research batch pass.

rightsLegend.pd: Public domain in the US. Government work (FSA/OWI etc.) or published ~1930 or earlier. Archival-res source usually available.
rightsLegend.mixed: The corpus mixes open and protected works. Prefer open files, then apply the documented per-image educational fair-use policy.
rightsLegend.copyrighted: Under active estate/agency. Apply the documented per-image educational fair-use policy and retain attribution.

sourceModeLegend.standard: The sourced image count meets minImages.
sourceModeLegend.limited: An exhaustive current-policy pass could not meet minImages; build a disclosed prose-led chapter with the real images that were usable, including zero.

stageLegend.sourced: sources.md is written and either raw/<slug>/ meets minImages or sourceMode is limited
stageLegend.built: ingested, overlays, proofs, chapter.mdx done; wired into site

_meta.queue: registry IS the queue. A prompt's active wave = the lowest wave containing photographers at its input stage. Waves are PD-first: 1=batch1, 2-4=remaining PD, 5=mixed, 6-9=copyrighted.

photographers.name: Nicéphore Niépce
photographers.note: First surviving photograph, View from the Window at Le Gras.

photographers.name: Louis Daguerre
photographers.note: Daguerreotype; Boulevard du Temple.

photographers.name: William Henry Fox Talbot
photographers.note: The Pencil of Nature — first commercial photobook.

photographers.name: Anna Atkins
photographers.note: Cyanotypes; first book illustrated with photographs.

photographers.name: Hippolyte Bayard
photographers.note: Self-Portrait as a Drowned Man — first staged photograph.

photographers.name: Hill & Adamson
photographers.note: Calotype portrait partnership.

photographers.name: Gustave Le Gray
photographers.note: Combination-printed seascapes.

photographers.name: Roger Fenton
photographers.note: Crimean War; Valley of the Shadow of Death.

photographers.name: Nadar (Félix Tournachon)
photographers.note: Portraiture; first aerial photographs.

photographers.name: Mathew Brady
photographers.note: US Civil War; government/pre-1930.

photographers.name: Alexander Gardner
photographers.note: Gardner's Photographic Sketch Book of the War.

photographers.name: Timothy O'Sullivan
photographers.note: Civil War + Western geological surveys.

photographers.name: Carleton Watkins

photographers.name: William Henry Jackson

photographers.name: Eadweard Muybridge

photographers.name: Étienne-Jules Marey

photographers.name: Felice Beato

photographers.name: John Thomson
photographers.note: Street Life in London; China (colonial lens — note in prose).

photographers.name: Jacob Riis

photographers.note: Child-labor reform; Empire State construction. Work pre-1930.

photographers.name: Julia Margaret Cameron
photographers.note: Soft-focus allegorical portraits.

photographers.name: Peter Henry Emerson
photographers.note: Naturalistic Photography; Norfolk Broads.

photographers.name: Frederick H. Evans
photographers.note: Cathedral interiors; platinum print. Work pre-1930.

photographers.name: Gertrude Käsebier
photographers.note: Photo-Secession; motherhood themes.

photographers.name: Clarence H. White
photographers.note: Pictorialist tonal compositions.

photographers.name: Edward S. Curtis
photographers.note: Ethnographic project; staging/romanticization — note in prose.

photographers.name: Frances Benjamin Johnston
photographers.note: Frontal geometric documentary.

photographers.name: Alvin Langdon Coburn
photographers.note: Vortographs — first abstract photographs. Work pre-1930.

photographers.name: Karl Blossfeldt
photographers.note: Urformen der Kunst (1928) — plant forms, pure geometry. Ideal overlay test.

photographers.name: Alfred Stieglitz
photographers.note: The Steerage (1907) PD; later Equivalents under estate.

photographers.name: Eugène Atget

photographers.name: Edward Steichen
photographers.note: The Pond—Moonlight (1904) PD; later fashion/portrait under estate.

photographers.name: Paul Strand
photographers.note: 1916 work (Wall Street, Blind Woman) PD; later under estate.

photographers.name: László Moholy-Nagy
photographers.note: Bauhaus photograms; 1920s work partly PD.

photographers.name: Alexander Rodchenko
photographers.note: Constructivist diagonals; 1920s work, Russian rights caveat.

photographers.name: Germaine Krull
photographers.note: Métal (1928) — industrial abstraction.

photographers.name: Albert Renger-Patzsch
photographers.note: Die Welt ist schön (1928) — New Objectivity.

photographers.name: August Sander

photographers.name: Man Ray
photographers.note: Rayographs; estate aggressive despite 1920s dates.

photographers.name: André Kertész

photographers.name: Jacques-Henri Lartigue

photographers.name: Dorothea Lange

photographers.name: Walker Evans
photographers.note: FSA frontality/symmetry — cleanest overlay case. Batch-1.

photographers.name: Gordon Parks
photographers.note: American Gothic (Ella Watson) is FSA PD; huge later career under foundation.

photographers.name: Arthur Rothstein
photographers.note: Dust Bowl; steer skull controversy.

photographers.name: Ben Shahn
photographers.note: FSA street/social documentary.

photographers.name: Russell Lee
photographers.note: Frontal flash interiors; Pie Town.

photographers.name: Marion Post Wolcott
photographers.note: Segregation-era South.

photographers.name: Jack Delano
photographers.note: Early FSA color — strong for a color-composition chapter.

photographers.name: John Vachon
photographers.note: Quiet Midwestern geometry.

photographers.name: Berenice Abbott
photographers.source: NYPL / Museum of the City of New York
photographers.note: Changing New York was WPA/FAP — parts PD; rights complex.

photographers.name: Margaret Bourke-White
photographers.note: Industrial scale; first LIFE cover.

photographers.name: Robert Capa
photographers.note: D-Day; Falling Soldier.

photographers.name: W. Eugene Smith
photographers.note: The photo essay; Minamata; Country Doctor.

photographers.name: Henri Cartier-Bresson
photographers.note: The decisive moment; geometry of the street. No recorded settings (film).

photographers.name: Alfred Eisenstaedt
photographers.note: V-J Day in Times Square.

photographers.name: Weegee (Arthur Fellig)
photographers.note: Flash noir; NYC crime scenes.

photographers.name: Yousuf Karsh
photographers.note: Studio portrait lighting; Churchill.

photographers.name: Brassaï
photographers.note: Paris by Night — long-exposure atmosphere.

photographers.name: Robert Doisneau
photographers.note: Le Baiser; humanist Paris.

photographers.name: Josef Koudelka
photographers.note: Prague 1968; Gypsies; Exiles.

photographers.name: Cecil Beaton
photographers.source: Condé Nast / Sotheby's Cecil Beaton Studio Archive
photographers.note: Theatrical fashion and society portraits.

photographers.name: Horst P. Horst
photographers.note: Mainbocher Corset; classical studio light.

photographers.name: George Hurrell
photographers.note: Hollywood glamour lighting.

photographers.name: Irving Penn
photographers.source: The Irving Penn Foundation / Condé Nast
photographers.note: Corner portraits; still life; controlled negative space.

photographers.name: Richard Avedon
photographers.note: White-seamless portraits; In the American West. Estate aggressive.

photographers.name: Helmut Newton
photographers.note: Provocative fashion; hard flash and scale.

photographers.name: Guy Bourdin
photographers.note: Radical color and cropping in fashion narrative.

photographers.name: Lillian Bassman
photographers.note: High-contrast, blurred, graphic fashion.

photographers.name: Annie Leibovitz
photographers.note: Conceptual celebrity portraiture. Aggressive rights.

photographers.name: Ansel Adams
photographers.source: The Ansel Adams Publishing Rights Trust
photographers.note: Zone System; Moonrise; Clearing Winter Storm. Aggressive rights.

photographers.name: Edward Weston
photographers.note: Peppers, nudes, Point Lobos — form as subject.

photographers.name: Imogen Cunningham
photographers.note: Botanicals; f/64. Some early work PD.

photographers.name: Minor White
photographers.note: Equivalence; sequences.

photographers.name: Wynn Bullock
photographers.note: Figure in landscape; luminous dark tones.

photographers.name: Eliot Porter
photographers.note: Dye-transfer color nature — intimate landscape.

photographers.name: Bill Brandt
photographers.note: High-contrast nudes, nudes-in-landscape, British social scenes.

photographers.name: Josef Sudek
photographers.note: The window of his studio; poetic Prague still life.

photographers.name: Sebastião Salgado
photographers.note: Workers; Genesis — epic tonal scale.

photographers.name: Hiroshi Sugimoto
photographers.note: Seascapes, Theaters — minimalism and time.

photographers.name: Lisette Model
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Close, confrontational street; taught Arbus.

photographers.name: Helen Levitt
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: NYC street children; early color.

photographers.name: Saul Leiter
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Pioneering color; reflections, layering, obstruction.

photographers.name: Robert Frank
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: The Americans — the anti-composed frame.

photographers.name: Diane Arbus
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Frontal square portraits. Estate extremely aggressive.

photographers.name: Garry Winogrand
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Tilted horizon; dense street energy.

photographers.name: Lee Friedlander
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Reflections, self in frame, social landscape.

photographers.name: Vivian Maier
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Rolleiflex street/self-portraits. Copyright famously tangled.

photographers.name: William Eggleston
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Legitimized color; the democratic frame; dye-transfer red.

photographers.name: Stephen Shore
photographers.group: IX. Street, the Frame & American Color
photographers.note: Uncommon Places — deadpan color; New Topographics.

photographers.name: Manuel Álvarez Bravo
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Poetic Mexican modernism.

photographers.name: Graciela Iturbide
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Nuestra Señora de las Iguanas; Juchitán.

photographers.name: Seydou Keïta
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Bamako studio portraits; pattern-on-pattern.

photographers.name: Malick Sidibé
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Bamako nightlife and studio joy.

photographers.name: Daido Moriyama
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Are-Bure-Boke; Provoke-era grain and blur.

photographers.name: Shomei Tomatsu
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Postwar Japan; Nagasaki.

photographers.name: Fan Ho
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.source: Fan Ho Estate / Blue Lotus Gallery
photographers.note: 1950s–60s Hong Kong; shafts of light, strong geometry — excellent overlay subject.

photographers.name: Raghu Rai
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: India; Bhopal; dense humanist frames.

photographers.name: David Goldblatt
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Apartheid-era South Africa; structures and values.

photographers.name: Bernd & Hilla Becher
photographers.group: X. Global Masters & the Postwar World
photographers.note: Industrial typologies; the grid — founders of the Düsseldorf School.

photographers.name: Jerry Uelsmann
photographers.source: Jerry Uelsmann Estate / Harn Museum / Met
photographers.note: Darkroom combination printing; seamless dream spaces assembled from multiple negatives.

photographers.name: Sandy Skoglund
photographers.source: Artist / Ryan Lee Gallery / museum collections
photographers.note: Hand-built monochrome installations photographed as uncanny domestic worlds.

photographers.name: Cindy Sherman
photographers.note: Staged self-transformation, prosthetics, costume, and constructed photographic identities.

photographers.name: Jeff Wall
photographers.note: Large-scale cinematic tableaux, reconstructed events, and digitally assembled scenes.

photographers.name: Gregory Crewdson
photographers.note: Elaborately lit suburban tableaux produced with the scale and control of cinema.

photographers.name: James Casebere
photographers.note: Photographs of fabricated architectural models where scale, light, and reality remain unstable.

photographers.name: Thomas Demand
photographers.source: Artist / Matthew Marks Gallery / museum collections
photographers.note: Life-size paper reconstructions photographed before the models are destroyed.

photographers.name: David Levinthal
photographers.note: Toy-scale staged histories that exploit photographic blur and apparent realism.

photographers.name: Bernard Faucon
photographers.source: Artist / Maison Européenne de la Photographie / galleries
photographers.note: Staged childhood dream worlds populated by mannequins, fire, summer light, and memory.

photographers.name: Teun Hocks
photographers.note: Painted and staged self-portraits turning ordinary predicaments into deadpan theater.

photographers.name: Lori Nix
photographers.note: Meticulously built miniature interiors photographed as posthuman ruins.

photographers.name: JeeYoung Lee
photographers.source: Artist / Gallery Now / museum and festival records
photographers.note: Surreal room-sized sets constructed by hand and photographed without digital compositing.

photographers.name: Nancy Burson
photographers.note: Pioneering computer-generated composite portraits and algorithmic faces.

photographers.name: Joan Fontcuberta
photographers.source: Artist / Galeria Àngels Barcelona / museum collections
photographers.note: Elaborate photographic fictions, fabricated archives, and critiques of image truth.

photographers.name: Andreas Gursky
photographers.note: Monumental digitally refined spaces organized by repetition, scale, and global systems.

photographers.name: Thomas Ruff
photographers.note: Digital enlargement, compression, found imagery, and image-generation systems test photography's limits.

photographers.name: Beate Gütschow
photographers.note: Digitally constructed landscapes and cities assembled from many photographic fragments.

photographers.name: Filip Dujardin
photographers.note: Impossible architecture made plausible through restrained digital photomontage.

photographers.name: Craig Kalpakjian
photographers.note: Early photoreal computer-generated interiors with no camera-world counterpart.

photographers.name: Loretta Lux
photographers.source: Artist / Yossi Milo / MoMA and MoCP collections
photographers.note: Digitally altered child portraits combining photographed figures with painted, uncanny spaces.

photographers.name: Ruud van Empel
photographers.note: Hyper-detailed portraits and forests composited from separately photographed fragments.

photographers.name: Maggie Taylor
photographers.source: Artist / Harn Museum / gallery and museum collections
photographers.note: Layered digital collages combining scans, photographs, drawing, and found Victorian material.

photographers.name: Inez & Vinoodh
photographers.source: Artists / Kunstmuseum Den Haag / official archive
photographers.note: Early digital distortion and fashion-image intervention used to destabilize bodies and identity.

photographers.name: Vibeke Tandberg
photographers.note: Early digital doubles and altered self-portraits probe unstable identity and authorship.

photographers.name: Erik Johansson
photographers.note: Photographed elements composited into seamless, physically impossible visual ideas.

photographers.name: David LaChapelle
photographers.source: Artist / Fotografiska / museum and gallery records
photographers.note: Hyper-saturated staged worlds, elaborate sets, and post-production shaped by pop and art history.

photographers.name: Tim Walker
photographers.source: Artist / Victoria and Albert Museum / official archive
photographers.note: Fantasy fashion narratives built through extravagant props, sets, scale, and controlled finishing.

photographers.name: Eugenio Recuenco
photographers.note: Painterly cinematic tableaux combining constructed sets, costume, allegory, and digital finishing.

photographers.name: Kirsty Mitchell
photographers.source: Artist / Wonderland official archive / exhibition records
photographers.note: Handmade costumes and sets expanded through meticulous fantasy post-production.

photographers.name: Brooke Shaden
photographers.source: Artist / official archive / exhibition and publication records
photographers.note: Dark surreal self-portraits built through compositing, texture, symbolic props, and tonal control.

photographers.name: Flóra Borsi
photographers.note: Surreal self-portrait transformations created through precise digital editing and visual substitution.

photographers.name: Miss Aniela (Natalie Dybisz)
photographers.source: Artist / official archive / exhibition and publication records
photographers.note: Surreal fashion and self-portrait composites mixing photographed figures with impossible environments.

photographers.name: Aydın Büyüktaş
photographers.source: Artist / official Flatland archive / exhibition records
photographers.note: Drone and ground photographs mapped into folded cityscapes with impossible curved perspective.

photographers.name: Julia Fullerton-Batten
photographers.source: Artist / National Portrait Gallery / museum collections
photographers.note: Cinematic staged narratives using elaborate locations, lighting, scale, and controlled post-production.

photographers.name: Tom Chambers
photographers.source: Artist / Photo-Eye / gallery and publication records
photographers.note: Narrative photomontages blending separately photographed and hand-drawn elements into magic realism.

photographers.name: Alex Prager
photographers.note: Cinematic crowd tableaux, melodramatic color, and post-production that heighten constructed unease.
